stem: Determine whether the following improper integral is convergent or divergent. If it is convergent, evaluate it.

A particle is moving along a straight line. For time $t \geq 0$, the velocity of the particle is given by $v(t) = 3t^2 - 12$. Let $b$ be an arbitrary number greater than 10. Find the \emph{total distance} travelled by the particle from time $t = 0$ to time $t = b$. Your answer should be an expression involving $b$.
The triangle whose vertices have $(x,y)$ coordinates $(0,1)$, $(1,0)$ and $(2,0)$ is rotated around the vertical line $x = -1$ to form a solid of revolution.

parts.label: a
parts.question_text: Using WASHERS, set up a definite integral (or the sum of two definite integrals if necessary) for the volume of this solid of revolution. DO NOT EVALUATE THE INTEGRAL(S).

parts.label: b
parts.question_text: Using SHELLS, set up a definite integral (or the sum of two definite integrals if necessary) for the volume of this solid of revolution. DO NOT EVALUATE THE INTEGRAL(S).

stem: The curve $x = \sqrt{\sin^{-1} y}$ for $0 \leq y \leq 1$ is rotated around the $y$-axis to form a container. The container is filled with a fluid that weighs $40 \text{ lb/ft}^3$. Length units for $x$ and $y$ are in feet.

parts.label: a
parts.question_text: Set up a definite integral (with respect to $y$) for the work (in ft-lb) required to empty the container by pumping all of the fluid to the top of the container. (Note: Do not use the acceleration due to gravity; pounds are already a unit of force.) IN THIS PART, DO NOT EVALUATE THE INTEGRAL YET.
parts.diagram_description: A diagram showing a coordinate system with y-axis vertical and x-axis horizontal. The y-axis ranges from 0 to 1.0, and the x-axis ranges from approximately -1.0 to 1.0. A curve representing $x = \sqrt{\sin^{-1} y}$ is drawn, starting at the origin and curving upward and to the right, reaching a maximum x-value of approximately 1.0 at y = 1.0. The region enclosed by the curve and the y-axis forms the cross-section of the container.

parts.label: b
parts.question_text: Now evaluate the integral in part (a). Give your answer in exact form.

parts.label: a
parts.question_text: Set up a definite integral for the arclength of the curve $y = \frac{1}{3}x^3 + \frac{1}{2}x^2$ for $-2 \leq x \leq 2$. DO NOT EVALUATE THE INTEGRAL.

parts.label: b
parts.question_text: Use Simpson's rule with $n = 4$ subintervals to approximate the definite integral in part (a). Give your answer in exact form.

stem: Find the $x$-coordinate $\bar{x}$ of the centroid (center of mass) of the region in the first quadrant inside the circle of radius $r$ centered at the origin. (The region is a quarter of a circle.) Give your answer in exact form. Your answer will involve $r$.
Find the solution of the initial value problem

parts.question_text: $\frac{dy}{dx} = 2x e^{-\tan(y)} \cos^2(y)$, $y(0) = \frac{\pi}{4}$. Give your answer in the form $y = f(x)$.

stem: A person borrows \$20,000 and repays the loan at the rate of $A$ dollars per year. The lender charges interest on the loan at a rate of 5% per year. Assuming that payments are made continuously and that interest is compounded continuously, the amount $y(t)$ of money (in dollars) owed $t$ years after the loan is made satisfies the differential equation $\frac{dy}{dt} = \frac{1}{20}y - A$ and the initial condition $y(0) = 20,000$.

parts.label: a
parts.question_text: Solve this initial-value problem (the differential equation together with the initial condition) to get a formula for $y(t)$. Your answer will involve both $t$ and $A$.

parts.label: b
parts.question_text: Find the value of $A$ which makes $y(10) = 0$ (so the loan is paid off in 10 years). Give your answer in exact form.
